behaviors.description: Valid JSON payloads containing markdown-heavy strings parse successfully and proceed through normal hook input handling.
behaviors.justification: Real risk is not JSON.parse itself but local handoff correctness: parser output must flow through hook-input handling without being misclassified due to markdown-like content (` ``` `, backticks, `$vars`). A pure unit test on one function can miss wiring/state bugs between parser and handler.
behaviors.test_description: Run an in-process test that feeds a valid JSON stdin-equivalent payload with markdown-heavy fields through the same parser->hook-input handling pipeline used in production (no malformed suffix). Assert parse success, no `json_parse_failed` trace, and normal handler path/result.

behaviors.description: Truly malformed JSON payloads produce a `json_parse_failed` trace record with enough context to diagnose the malformed section.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is parse-error capture plus trace shaping/context extraction across local modules (parser wrapper + tracing). Mocking either side can hide missing/incorrect diagnostic context.
behaviors.test_description: Feed malformed JSON samples (e.g., broken quote, truncated object, invalid comma) into the parser->trace pipeline in-process. Assert a `json_parse_failed` trace is emitted and includes required context fields (error message/class + offset/snippet/correlation metadata) sufficient to locate malformed region.

behaviors.description: The local error-policy path maps parse failure to a handled result (no throw) and marks telemetry intent.
behaviors.justification: This is local policy wiring: parse exception must be translated into a handled result object and telemetry intent. Single-function unit tests can miss exception propagation or dropped telemetry flags across boundaries.
behaviors.test_description: Execute parse-failure input through parser + error-policy + telemetry-intent mapper in one in-process test. Assert no uncaught exception, returned handled/failure result shape is correct, and telemetry-intent marker is set.

behaviors.description: In real process execution, malformed stdin does not crash the hook process and still emits the expected trace signal before exit.
behaviors.justification: Requires real subprocess/OS stdin and process lifecycle semantics. In-process mocks can miss crash-on-read, buffering, signal ordering, and exit-code behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Spawn the actual hook executable as a subprocess, write malformed JSON to stdin, close stdin, and wait for exit. Assert process does not hard-crash, emits `json_parse_failed` trace signal before termination, and exits with expected handled code/contract.

behaviors.description: A real hook invocation path (stdin -> parser -> trace sink) produces the expected trace artifact when malformed input is supplied.
behaviors.justification: `stdin -> parser -> trace sink` on real invocation crosses process and likely OS/file/pipe behavior. Mocked sinks can hide serialization/flush/path issues that only appear in real execution.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the hook via its real entrypoint with malformed stdin and configured real trace sink (e.g., file/stdout collector used in runtime). Assert produced artifact contains `json_parse_failed` record with required fields and is persisted/flushed before process exit.

behaviors.description: Regression tests distinguish malformed-JSON failures from valid-markdown payloads, preventing false attribution to markdown content itself.
behaviors.justification: Need paired-path verification through shared local pipeline to prove classifier/branching behavior is correct: valid markdown-heavy JSON must pass while structurally malformed JSON must fail. Unit-only tests are prone to overfitting isolated helpers.
behaviors.test_description: Create a regression table test that runs both classes through the same parser->handling->trace path: (a) valid JSON with markdown-heavy content and (b) malformed JSON variants. Assert only malformed cases emit `json_parse_failed`; valid markdown cases take normal path with no parse-failure trace.
